reasoning: `always_unfold` is external_body bridging `always(p).satisfied_by(ex)` — which evaluates a `TempPred::new` closure containing a forall — to a bare forall over `p.satisfied_by(ex.suffix(i))`. Unverified trust assumption resolving the intensional closure gap.
`always(¬p) ⟹ ¬eventually(p)` is a standard temporal logic tautology (∀i.¬p(i) ⟹ ¬∃i.p(i)). This is a correct consequence of `always_unfold` — no new trust surface beyond φ1.
Extracting `¬p` at a specific index from `always(¬p)` is a direct instantiation of φ1 — standard universal elimination. No new trust surface.
`always(¬(¬p)) ⟹ ¬eventually(¬p)` is a correct instantiation of φ2 with `not(p)` substituted for `p`. Standard temporal logic, no new trust surface.
`always(¬p) ∧ eventually(p) ⟹ false` is a standard temporal logic tautology — these premises are genuinely contradictory. This is expected behavior, not a spec gap.
